`Biomarker ~ Determinant + GA_weeks + (GA quadratic/cubic component) + (1 | id_bisc)`

`Biomarker ~ Determinant * (GA_weeks + (GA quadratic/cubic component)) + (1 | id_bisc)`


<span style="color:#2E8B57; font-weight:bold;">
3) Are the determinants associated with the trajectory of the biomarkers during prengnacy and post-partum?
</span>

<span style="font-weight:bold;">
Longitudinal - pregnancy and post-partum: (v1-v4)
</span>

`Biomarker ~ Determinant + Visit + (1 | id_bisc)`

**RESULTS**:

**Hospital of recruitment**: 

1) ✔ Higher <span style="color:#7570b3; font-weight:bold;">GFAP</span> between SJD and StPau at V1 and V2.

2) ✔✔ During pregnancy, SJD shows overall higher <span style="color:#7570b3; font-weight:bold;">GFAP</span> levels compared with StPau. <span style="color:#7570b3; font-weight:bold;">GFAP</span> trajectories differ depending on the hospital (Maternitat has sharper increase).

3) ✔ Across visits, SJD shows overall higher <span style="color:#7570b3; font-weight:bold;">GFAP</span> levels compared with StPau.

**Baseline age of mother**: 

1) ✔ Older mothers have higher <span style="color:#1b9e77; font-weight:bold;">pTau217</span> (v4), higher <span style="color:#d95f02; font-weight:bold;">NfL</span> (V1, V2, V3, V4) and higher <span style="color:#7570b3; font-weight:bold;">GFAP</span> (V2).

2) ✔✖ During pregnancy, higher maternal age was significantly associated with higher <span style="color:#d95f02; font-weight:bold;">NfL</span>  and <span style="color:#7570b3; font-weight:bold;">GFAP</span>.

3) ✔ Across visits, higher maternal age was significantly associated with higher <span style="color:#d95f02; font-weight:bold;">NfL</span>.

**Ethnics**: 

1) ✔ "Not European" have higher <span style="color:#7570b3; font-weight:bold;">GFAP</span> (V1, V4).

2) ✖✔ During pregnancy, different trajectories are found for <span style="color:#7570b3; font-weight:bold;">GFAP</span>.

3) ✖

**BMI**: 

1) ✔ Higher <span style="color:#d95f02; font-weight:bold;">NfL</span>  is associated with lower BMI (V4).

2) ✖✖

3) ✖

**Education**: 

1) ✖

2) ✖✖

3) ✖


**COVID groups**: 

1) ✖

2) ✖✔ During pregnancy, different trajectories are found for <span style="color:#7570b3; font-weight:bold;">GFAP</span> (Group 2).

3) ✖

**Sex of the child**: 

1) ✖

2) ✖✖

3) ✖

**Parity**: 

1) ✔ Higher <span style="color:#d95f02; font-weight:bold;">NfL</span> in multiparous women (V4). 

2) ✖✖

3) ✖

**APOE**: 

1) ✔ Higher <span style="color:#1b9e77; font-weight:bold;">pTau217</span> in APOE3/3 regarding APOE2/3 (V3). Higher <span style="color:#d95f02; font-weight:bold;">NfL</span>  in APOE4/4 regarding APOE2/3 across visits. (Consider low "n")

2) ✔✔ During pregnancy, APOE4/4 show significantly higher <span style="color:#d95f02; font-weight:bold;">NfL</span>  levels during pregnancy. Different trajectories are found for each genotype. (Consider low "n")

3) ✖

**Delivery type**: 

1) ✖

2) ✖✖

3) ✖

_______________________________________________________________________

```{r formula, results='hide', message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
run_biomarker_variable <- function(df, variable, biomarker){

  library(dplyr)
  library(lme4)
  library(lmerTest)
  library(broom.mixed)
  library(kableExtra)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(scales)
  library(tibble)
  library(see)

  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      id_bisc = factor(id_bisc),
      Visit = factor(Visit, levels = c("v1","v2","v3","v4"))
    )

  # =========================================================
  # GLOBAL FACTOR + COLOR SETUP
  # =========================================================

  is_cat <- is.factor(df[[variable]]) || is.character(df[[variable]])

  if (is_cat) {

    df[[variable]] <- factor(df[[variable]])
    all_levels <- levels(df[[variable]])

    palette_vals <- setNames(
      hue_pal()(length(all_levels)),
      all_levels
    )
  }

  ga_term <- if (biomarker == "log_NFL") {
    "GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2) + I(GA_weeks^3)"
  } else {
    "GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2)"
  }

  # =========================================================
  # TIDY MODEL OUTPUT
  # =========================================================

  tidy_model <- function(model){

    tab <- broom.mixed::tidy(model, effects = "fixed") %>%
      filter(term != "(Intercept)")

    if ("effect" %in% colnames(tab)) {
      tab <- tab %>% select(-effect)
    }

    tab %>%
      mutate(
        estimate = round(estimate, 3),
        std.error = round(std.error, 3),
        p_fmt = ifelse(
          p.value < 0.001,
          "<0.001",
          sprintf("%.3f", p.value)
        ),
        signif = case_when(
          p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
          p.value < 0.01 ~ "**",
          p.value < 0.05 ~ "*",
          TRUE ~ ""
        )
      )
  }

  # =========================================================
  # GET N USED IN MODEL
  # =========================================================

  get_model_n <- function(model){

    mf <- model.frame(model)

    tibble(
      n = nrow(mf),
      n_id = if ("id_bisc" %in% names(mf)) {
        dplyr::n_distinct(mf$id_bisc)
      } else {
        NA_integer_
      }
    )
  }

  # =========================================================
  # SAFE MODEL WRAPPER
  # =========================================================

  safe_model <- function(formula_txt, data, random = FALSE){

    fml <- as.formula(formula_txt)

    if (random) {

      if (dplyr::n_distinct(data$id_bisc) < nrow(data)) {

        lmer(fml, data = data, REML = TRUE)

      } else {

        lm(
          as.formula(
            gsub("\\+ \\(1 \\| id_bisc\\)", "", formula_txt)
          ),
          data = data
        )
      }

    } else {

      lm(fml, data = data)
    }
  }

  visits <- c("v1","v2","v3","v4")

  # =========================================================
  # CROSS-SECTIONAL MODELS
  # =========================================================

  cat("\n\n<div style='margin:20px 0;'><hr></div>\n\n")

  for (v in visits) {

    df_v <- df %>%
      filter(Visit == v)

    if (is_cat) {
      df_v[[variable]] <- factor(
        df_v[[variable]],
        levels = all_levels
      )
    }

    formula_cross <- paste0(
      biomarker,
      " ~ ",
      variable,
      " + GA_weeks + (1 | id_bisc)"
    )

    model_cross <- safe_model(
      formula_cross,
      df_v,
      random = TRUE
    )

    n_info <- get_model_n(model_cross)

    tab <- tidy_model(model_cross) %>%
      mutate(
        Model = paste0("Cross ", v),
        n = n_info$n,
        n_id = n_info$n_id
      )

    cat("\n\n**Visit:", v, "**\n\n")
    cat("```r\n", formula_cross, "\n```\n\n")

    print(
      tab %>%
        select(
          Model,
          n,
          n_id,
          term,
          estimate,
          std.error,
          p_fmt,
          signif
        ) %>%
        kable(format = "html") %>%
        kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
    )

    # =====================================================
    # CROSS-SECTIONAL PLOT
    # =====================================================

    if (is_cat) {

      p <- ggplot(
        df_v,
        aes(
          x = .data[[variable]],
          y = .data[[biomarker]],
          fill = .data[[variable]]
        )
      ) +
        geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.5) +
        geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.3) +
        scale_fill_manual(
          values = palette_vals,
          drop = FALSE
        ) +
        theme_minimal() +
        labs(
          x = variable,
          y = biomarker
        ) +
        theme(
          legend.position = "none"
        )

    } else {

      p <- ggplot(
        df_v,
        aes(
          x = .data[[variable]],
          y = .data[[biomarker]]
        )
      ) +
        geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
        geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +
        theme_minimal() +
        labs(
          x = variable,
          y = biomarker
        )
    }

    print(p)
  }

  # =========================================================
  # PREGNANCY MODEL
  # =========================================================

cat(
  "\n\n<div style='margin:30px 0;'><hr style='border:2px solid #999'></div>\n\n"
)

cat("**📊 LONGITUDINAL: PREGNANCY MODEL (v1–v3)**\n\n")

df_preg <- df %>%
  filter(Visit %in% c("v1","v2","v3"))

if (is_cat) {

  df_preg[[variable]] <- factor(
    df_preg[[variable]],
    levels = all_levels
  )
}

# -----------------------------------------
# MAIN EFFECT MODEL
# -----------------------------------------

formula_preg <- paste0(
  biomarker,
  " ~ ",
  variable,
  " + ",
  ga_term,
  " + (1 | id_bisc)"
)

m_preg <- safe_model(
  formula_preg,
  df_preg,
  random = TRUE
)

n_info <- get_model_n(m_preg)

tab_preg <- tidy_model(m_preg) %>%
  mutate(
    Model = "Pregnancy",
    n = n_info$n,
    n_id = n_info$n_id
  )

cat("\n\n```r\n", formula_preg, "\n```\n\n")

print(
  tab_preg %>%
    select(
      Model,
      n,
      n_id,
      term,
      estimate,
      std.error,
      p_fmt,
      signif
    ) %>%
    kable(format = "html") %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
)

# =========================================================
# INTERACTION MODEL
# =========================================================

cat("\n\n**📈 LONGITUDINAL: PREGNANCY MODEL (v1–v3) - INTERACTION WITH GA_weeks**\n\n")

formula_preg_int <- paste0(
  biomarker,
  " ~ ",
  variable,
  " * (GA_weeks + I(GA_weeks^2)",
  if (biomarker == "log_NFL") " + I(GA_weeks^3)" else "",
  ") + (1 | id_bisc)"
)

m_preg_int <- safe_model(
  formula_preg_int,
  df_preg,
  random = TRUE
)

tab_preg_int <- tidy_model(m_preg_int) %>%
  mutate(
    Model = "Pregnancy Interaction",
    n = n_info$n,
    n_id = n_info$n_id
  )

cat("\n\n```r\n", formula_preg_int, "\n```\n\n")

print(
  tab_preg_int %>%
    select(
      Model,
      n,
      n_id,
      term,
      estimate,
      std.error,
      p_fmt,
      signif
    ) %>%
    kable(format = "html") %>%
    kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
)
  # =====================================================
  # SPAGHETTI PLOT
  # =====================================================

  p1 <- ggplot(
    df_preg,
    aes(
      GA_weeks,
      .data[[biomarker]],
      group = id_bisc
    )
  ) +
    geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
    theme_minimal()

  if (is_cat) {
    p1 <- p1 + aes(color = .data[[variable]])
  }

  print(p1)

  # =====================================================
  # SMOOTH PLOT
  # =====================================================

  p2 <- ggplot(
    df_preg,
    aes(
      GA_weeks,
      .data[[biomarker]]
    )
  ) +
    geom_smooth(
      method = "lm",
      se = TRUE,
      linewidth = 1.2
    ) +
    theme_minimal()

  if (is_cat) {
    p2 <- p2 + aes(color = .data[[variable]])
  }

  print(p2)

  # =========================================================
  # POSTPARTUM MODEL
  # =========================================================

  cat(
    "\n\n<div style='margin:30px 0;'><hr style='border:2px solid #999'></div>\n\n"
  )

  cat("**📊 LONGITUDINAL: POSTPARTUM MODEL (v1–v4)**\n\n")

  df_post <- df

  if (is_cat) {

    df_post[[variable]] <- factor(
      df_post[[variable]],
      levels = all_levels
    )
  }

  formula_post <- paste0(
    biomarker,
    " ~ ",
    variable,
    " + Visit + (1 | id_bisc)"
  )

  m_post <- safe_model(
    formula_post,
    df_post,
    random = TRUE
  )

  n_info <- get_model_n(m_post)

  tab_post <- tidy_model(m_post) %>%
    mutate(
      Model = "Postpartum",
      n = n_info$n,
      n_id = n_info$n_id
    )

  cat("\n\n```r\n", formula_post, "\n```\n\n")

  print(
    tab_post %>%
      select(
        Model,
        n,
        n_id,
        term,
        estimate,
        std.error,
        p_fmt,
        signif
      ) %>%
      kable(format = "html") %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  # =====================================================
  # POSTPARTUM SPAGHETTI PLOT
  # =====================================================

  p3 <- ggplot(
    df_post,
    aes(
      GA_weeks,
      .data[[biomarker]],
      group = id_bisc
    )
  ) +
    geom_line(alpha = 0.3) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.4) +
    theme_minimal()

  if (is_cat) {
    p3 <- p3 + aes(color = .data[[variable]])
  }

  print(p3)

  # =====================================================
  # POSTPARTUM SMOOTH PLOT
  # =====================================================

  p4 <- ggplot(
    df_post,
    aes(
      GA_weeks,
      .data[[biomarker]]
    )
  ) +
    geom_smooth(
      method = "lm",
      se = TRUE,
      linewidth = 1.2
    ) +
    theme_minimal()

  if (is_cat) {
    p4 <- p4 + aes(color = .data[[variable]])
  }

  print(p4)

  invisible(
    list(
      pregnancy = m_preg,
      postpartum = m_post
    )
  )
}
```


#### Hospital of recruitment {.tabset}

**Result**: No general significant differences between biomarker levels across hospital of recruitment. Found a trend: higher GFAP between SJD - StPau (v1, v2 and longitudinal).

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r hospital ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "hosp_recruit_m_12w", "log_pTau217")
```



##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r hospital nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "hosp_recruit_m_12w", "log_NFL")
```



##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r hospital gfap, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "hosp_recruit_m_12w", "log_GFAP")
```



#### Maternal age {.tabset}

**Result**: Older participants may have higher NfL (trend on GFAP).

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r age ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "age_12w_m", "log_pTau217")
```


##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r age nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "age_12w_m", "log_NFL")
```


##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r age gfap, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "age_12w_m", "log_GFAP")
```



#### Ethnics {.tabset}

**Result**: No general significant association between biomarkers and ethnic groups. Found a trend: higher GFAP in not European participants (v1 and v4).


##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r ethnics ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "ethnicity_m_2cat", "log_pTau217")
```

##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r ethnics nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "ethnicity_m_2cat", "log_NFL")
```


##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r ethnics gfap, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "ethnicity_m_2cat", "log_GFAP")
```


#### BMI {.tabset}

**Result**: No general significant association between biomarkers and pre-pregnancy BMI. Higher NfL found in participants with lower BMI (v4).

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r bmi ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "bmi_m_12w", "log_pTau217")
```

##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r bmi nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "bmi_m_12w", "log_NFL")
```

##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r bmi gfap, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "bmi_m_12w", "log_GFAP")
```


#### Education {.tabset}

**Result**: No significant association between biomarkers and education groups.

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r edu ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "educ_level_m_2cat", "log_pTau217")
```


##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r edu nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "educ_level_m_2cat", "log_NFL")
```


##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r edu gfap, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "educ_level_m_2cat", "log_GFAP")
```



#### COVID group {.tabset}

**Result**: No significant association between biomarkers and covid groups.

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r covidg ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "covid_frontier_m_3c", "log_pTau217")
```



##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r covidg nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "covid_frontier_m_3c", "log_NFL")
```


##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r covidg gfap, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "covid_frontier_m_3c", "log_GFAP")
```




#### Sex (child) {.tabset}

**Result**: No significant association between biomarkers and sex of the child.

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r sexchild ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "sex_0y_c", "log_pTau217")
```

##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r sexchild nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "sex_0y_c", "log_NFL")
```


##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r sexchild2 gfap, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "sex_0y_c", "log_GFAP")
```


#### Parity {.tabset}

**Result**: No general significant association between biomarkers and parity. Multiparous participants have higher NfL (v4).

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r parity ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "parity_m_2cat", "log_pTau217")
```

##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r parity nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "parity_m_2cat", "log_NFL")
```

##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r parity gfap, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "parity_m_2cat", "log_GFAP")
```


#### APOE {.tabset}

**Result**: Higher pTau217 in APOE3/3 regarding APOE2/3 (V3). Higher NfL in APOE4/4 regarding APOE2/3 across visits. During pregnancy, APOE4/4 show significantly higher NfL levels during pregnancy. Different trajectories are found for each genotype. (Consider low "n")

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r apoe ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "APOE_genotype", "log_pTau217")
#run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "APOE_e4_carrier", "log_pTau217")
```



##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r apoe nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "APOE_genotype", "log_NFL")
#run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "APOE_e4_carrier", "log_NFL")
```


##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r apoe gfap, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "APOE_genotype", "log_GFAP")
#run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "APOE_e4_carrier", "log_GFAP")
```



#### Delivery type {.tabset}

**Result**: No significant association between biomarkers and delivery type.

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r delivery ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "delivery_type_m_2cat", "log_pTau217")
```

##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r delivery nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "delivery_type_m_2cat", "log_NFL")
```


##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r delivery gfap, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_variable(long_df, "delivery_type_m_2cat", "log_GFAP")
```


### 2.3. Additional exploratory analyses {.tabset}

#### Univariate mixed model {.tabset}

This section explores potential covariates that may influence biomarker levels in the cohort, helping identify possible confounders for subsequent analyses (effect of maternal determinants and gestational age on biomarker levels across pregnancy and postpartum visits).

- **Test**: Mixed-effects model (lmer)  `Biomarker ~ Covariate + (1 | id_bisc)`

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r covptau217 univariate, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(purrr)
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest) 
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

covariates <- c(
  "GA_weeks", 
  "age_12w_m",
  "sex_0y_c",
  "bmi_m_12w",
  "ethnicity_m_2cat",
  "educ_level_m_2cat",
  "hosp_recruit_m_12w",
  "hospital_delivery",
  "covid_frontier_m_3c",
  "parity_m_2cat",
  "delivery_type_m_2cat"
)

bio <- "log_pTau217"

uni_results <- map_dfr(covariates, function(cov) {

  fml <- as.formula(paste0(bio, " ~ ", cov, " + (1 | id_bisc)"))

  mod <- lmer(fml, data = long_df, REML = TRUE)

  coef_table <- as.data.frame(summary(mod)$coefficients)
  coef_table$term <- rownames(coef_table)

  coef_table %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      covariate = cov,

      estimate = round(Estimate, 3),
      std.error = round(`Std. Error`, 3),
      statistic = round(`t value`, 2),
      p_raw = `Pr(>|t|)`,

      p.value_fmt = case_when(
        is.na(p_raw) ~ NA_character_,
        p_raw < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
        TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p_raw)
      ),

      signif = case_when(
        p_raw < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p_raw < 0.01  ~ "**",
        p_raw < 0.05  ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    )
})

uni_results %>%
  select(covariate, term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value_fmt, signif) %>%
  arrange(covariate) %>%
  kable(
    format = "html",
    escape = FALSE,
    col.names = c("Covariate", "Term", "Estimate", "Std.Err", "t value", "p value", "Signif")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
```
##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r covnfl univariate, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
bio <- "log_NFL"

uni_results <- map_dfr(covariates, function(cov) {

  fml <- as.formula(paste0(bio, " ~ ", cov, " + (1 | id_bisc)"))

  mod <- lmer(fml, data = long_df, REML = TRUE)

  coef_table <- as.data.frame(summary(mod)$coefficients)
  coef_table$term <- rownames(coef_table)

  coef_table %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      covariate = cov,

      estimate = round(Estimate, 3),
      std.error = round(`Std. Error`, 3),
      statistic = round(`t value`, 2),
      p_raw = `Pr(>|t|)`,

      p.value_fmt = case_when(
        is.na(p_raw) ~ NA_character_,
        p_raw < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
        TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p_raw)
      ),

      signif = case_when(
        p_raw < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p_raw < 0.01  ~ "**",
        p_raw < 0.05  ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    )
})

uni_results %>%
  select(covariate, term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value_fmt, signif) %>%
  arrange(covariate) %>%
  kable(
    format = "html",
    escape = FALSE,
    col.names = c("Covariate", "Term", "Estimate", "Std.Err", "t value", "p value", "Signif")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
```

##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r covgfap univariate, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
bio <- "log_GFAP"

uni_results <- map_dfr(covariates, function(cov) {

  fml <- as.formula(paste0(bio, " ~ ", cov, " + (1 | id_bisc)"))

  mod <- lmer(fml, data = long_df, REML = TRUE)

  coef_table <- as.data.frame(summary(mod)$coefficients)
  coef_table$term <- rownames(coef_table)

  coef_table %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(
      covariate = cov,

      estimate = round(Estimate, 3),
      std.error = round(`Std. Error`, 3),
      statistic = round(`t value`, 2),
      p_raw = `Pr(>|t|)`,

      p.value_fmt = case_when(
        is.na(p_raw) ~ NA_character_,
        p_raw < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
        TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p_raw)
      ),

      signif = case_when(
        p_raw < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p_raw < 0.01  ~ "**",
        p_raw < 0.05  ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    )
})

uni_results %>%
  select(covariate, term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value_fmt, signif) %>%
  arrange(covariate) %>%
  kable(
    format = "html",
    escape = FALSE,
    col.names = c("Covariate", "Term", "Estimate", "Std.Err", "t value", "p value", "Signif")
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, position = "left")
```

#### Multivariate mixed model {.tabset}

This section explores potential covariates that may influence biomarker levels in the cohort, helping identify possible confounders for subsequent analyses (effect of maternal determinants and gestational age on biomarker levels across pregnancy and postpartum visits).

- **Test**: Mixed-effects model (lmer)  `Biomarker ~ All covariates + (1 | id_bisc)`

##### pTau217 {.tabset}
```{r covptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
bio_colors <- c( "log_pTau217" = "#1b9e77", "log_NFL" = "#d95f02", "log_GFAP" = "#7570b3" )

run_biomarker_analysis <- function(
  bio,
  long_df,
  covariates,
  bio_colors,
  group_vars = c(
    "sex_0y_c",
    "ethnicity_m_2cat",
    "educ_level_m_2cat",
    "hosp_recruit_m_12w",
    "hospital_delivery",
    "covid_frontier_m_3c",
    "parity_m_2cat",
    "delivery_type_m_2cat"
  )
) {

  library(dplyr)
  library(broom.mixed)
  library(lme4)
  library(lmerTest)
  library(ggplot2)
  library(knitr)
  library(kableExtra)

  # -----------------------------
  # Visit order
  # -----------------------------
  long_df$Visit <- factor(long_df$Visit, levels = c("v1","v2","v3","v4"))
  contrasts(long_df$Visit) <- contr.treatment(4, base = 1)

  # -----------------------------
  # MODEL
  # -----------------------------
  formula_str <- paste0(
    bio, " ~ ",
    paste(covariates, collapse = " + "),
    "+ (1 | id_bisc)"
  )

  cat("<pre>Full model formula: ", formula_str, "</pre>\n")

  model <- lmer(as.formula(formula_str), data = long_df, REML = TRUE)

  tidy_res <- broom.mixed::tidy(model, effects = "fixed") %>%
    mutate(
      estimate = round(estimate, 3),
      std.error = round(std.error, 3),
      statistic = round(statistic, 2),
      p_raw = p.value,
      p.value = ifelse(p_raw < 0.001, "<0.001", sprintf("%.3f", p_raw)),
      signif = case_when(
        p_raw < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p_raw < 0.01  ~ "**",
        p_raw < 0.05  ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      )
    )

  # -----------------------------
  # TABLE
  # -----------------------------
  print(
    tidy_res %>%
      select(term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value, signif) %>%
      kable(format = "html", escape = FALSE,
            col.names = c("Covariate", "Estimate", "SE", "t", "p", "Sig.")) %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  col <- bio_colors[bio]

  # -----------------------------
  # GA plot
  # -----------------------------
  print(
    ggplot(long_df, aes(x = GA_weeks, y = .data[[bio]])) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.5, color = col) +
      geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black") +
      theme_minimal() +
      labs(title = paste0(bio, " vs GA"))
  )

  # -----------------------------
  # Visit plot
  # -----------------------------
  print(
    ggplot(long_df, aes(x = Visit, y = .data[[bio]])) +
      geom_boxplot(fill = col, alpha = 0.4, outlier.shape = NA) +
      geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5, color = col) +
      theme_minimal() +
      labs(title = paste0(bio, " by Visit"))
  )

  # -----------------------------
  # Covariate plots
  # -----------------------------
  for(cov in covariates[-1]){

    if(cov %in% colnames(long_df)){

      if(is.numeric(long_df[[cov]])){

        p <- ggplot(long_df, aes(x = .data[[cov]], y = .data[[bio]])) +
          geom_point(alpha = 0.6, color = col) +
          geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "black") +
          theme_minimal() +
          labs(title = paste0(bio, " vs ", cov))

      } else {

        p <- ggplot(long_df, aes(x = .data[[cov]], y = .data[[bio]])) +
          geom_boxplot(fill = col, alpha = 0.4) +
          geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5, color = col) +
          theme_minimal() +
          labs(title = paste0(bio, " by ", cov))
      }

      print(p)
    }
  }

# -----------------------------
# LONGITUDINAL GROUP PLOTS (TWO PER VARIABLE)
# -----------------------------
for(g in group_vars){

  if(g %in% colnames(long_df)){

    df_tmp <- long_df %>%
      mutate(group_var = as.factor(.data[[g]]))

    # =============================
    # PLOT 1: VISIT (structured time)
    # =============================
    print(

      ggplot(df_tmp,
             aes(x = Visit,
                 y = .data[[bio]],
                 group = id_bisc,
                 color = group_var)) +

        # spaghetti
        geom_line(alpha = 0.15) +

        geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 1) +

        # GROUP MEAN TRAJECTORY
        stat_summary(
          aes(group = group_var),
          fun = mean,
          geom = "line",
          linewidth = 1.3
        ) +

        stat_summary(
          aes(group = group_var),
          fun = mean,
          geom = "point",
          size = 2
        ) +

        theme_minimal() +

        labs(
          title = paste0(bio, " trajectories by ", g, " (Visit)"),
          x = "Visit",
          y = bio,
          color = g
        )
    )

    # =============================
    # PLOT 2: GA weeks (irregular time)
    # =============================
    print(

      ggplot(df_tmp,
             aes(x = GA_weeks,
                 y = .data[[bio]],
                 group = id_bisc,
                 color = group_var)) +

        # spaghetti ONLY (no smoothing)
        geom_line(alpha = 0.15) +

        geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 1) +

        theme_minimal() +

        labs(
          title = paste0(bio, " trajectories by ", g, " (GA weeks)"),
          x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
          y = bio,
          color = g
        )
    )
  }
}

} 

# RUN
run_biomarker_analysis("log_pTau217", long_df, covariates, bio_colors)
```

##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r covNFL, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_analysis("log_NFL", long_df, covariates, bio_colors)
```

##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r covGFAP, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
run_biomarker_analysis("log_GFAP", long_df, covariates, bio_colors)
```

#### Pre vs post birth biomarker acquisition (at v3) {.tabset}

```{r, results='hide', message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, include=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(broom)
library(kableExtra)
library(ggplot2)

# -----------------------------
# COLORS
# -----------------------------
bio_colors <- c(
  "log_pTau217" = "#1b9e77",
  "log_NFL"     = "#d95f02",
  "log_GFAP"    = "#7570b3"
)

# -----------------------------
# 1. V3 dataset
# -----------------------------
df_v3 <- long_df %>%
  filter(Visit == "v3") %>%
  mutate(
    bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y = factor(
      bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y,
      levels = c(0,1),
      labels = c("Before birth", "After birth")
    )
  )

# -----------------------------
# 2. FUNCTION
# -----------------------------
run_birth_timing_model <- function(df, biomarker, outcome = "bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y"){

  col <- bio_colors[biomarker]

  # -----------------------------
  # FORMULAS
  # -----------------------------
  f1 <- as.formula(paste(biomarker, "~", outcome))

  f2 <- as.formula(paste(
    biomarker, "~", outcome,
    "+ GA_weeks + age_12w_m + sex_0y_c + ethnicity_m_2cat + hosp_recruit_m_12w + parity_m_2cat + delivery_type_m_2cat"
  ))

  # -----------------------------
  # PRINT FORMULAS
  # -----------------------------
  cat("<pre>Unadjusted LR model (v3 dataset): ", deparse(f1), "</pre>\n")
  cat("<pre>Adjusted LR model (v3 dataset):   ", deparse(f2), "</pre>\n")

  # -----------------------------
  # MODELS
  # -----------------------------
  m1 <- lm(f1, data = df)
  m2 <- lm(f2, data = df)

  # -----------------------------
  # TIDY FUNCTION
  # -----------------------------
  tidy_fmt <- function(model, label){

    tidy(model) %>%
      filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
      mutate(
        Model = label,
        Beta = round(estimate, 3),
        SE = round(std.error, 3),
        t = round(statistic, 2),
        p_raw = p.value,

        p = case_when(
          p_raw < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
          TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p_raw)
        ),

        Sig = case_when(
          p_raw < 0.001 ~ "***",
          p_raw < 0.01  ~ "**",
          p_raw < 0.05  ~ "*",
          TRUE ~ ""
        )
      ) %>%
      select(Model, term, Beta, SE, t, p, Sig)
  }

  table <- bind_rows(
    tidy_fmt(m1, "Unadjusted"),
    tidy_fmt(m2, "Adjusted")
  )

  # -----------------------------
  # TABLE
  # -----------------------------
  print(
    table %>%
      kable(
        format = "html",
        escape = FALSE,
        col.names = c("Model", "Term", "Beta", "SE", "t", "p", "Sig.")
      ) %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  # -----------------------------
  # PLOT
  # -----------------------------
  print(
    ggplot(df, aes(x = .data[[outcome]], y = .data[[biomarker]])) +
      geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.4, fill = col, outlier.shape = NA) +
      geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5, color = col) +
      theme_minimal() +
      labs(
        x = "Birth timing (V3: before/after)",
        y = biomarker,
        title = paste(biomarker, "vs birth timing")
      )
  )

  return(list(unadjusted = m1, adjusted = m2))
}
########################################################################################### INVESTIGATE EACH GROUP
df_v3 <- long_df %>%
  filter(Visit == "v3") %>%
  mutate(
    birth_timing = case_when(
      bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y == 0 ~ "Before birth",
      bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y == 1 ~ "After birth",
      is.na(bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y) ~ "Missing"
    ),

    birth_timing = factor(
      birth_timing,
      levels = c("Before birth", "After birth", "Missing")
    )
  )

run_stratified_model <- function(df, biomarker, group_name){

  df_sub <- df %>% filter(birth_timing == group_name)

  if (nrow(df_sub) < 5) {
    cat("<b>Skipping", group_name, "- too few samples</b><br>")
    return(NULL)
  }

  f1 <- as.formula(paste(
    biomarker,
    "~ GA_weeks + age_12w_m + sex_0y_c +
       ethnicity_m_2cat + hosp_recruit_m_12w +
       parity_m_2cat + delivery_type_m_2cat"
  ))

  m1 <- lm(f1, data = df_sub)

  tidy_df <- broom::tidy(m1) %>%
    filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
    mutate(Group = group_name)

  # -----------------------------
  # ADD STARS
  # -----------------------------
  tidy_df <- tidy_df %>%
    mutate(
      Sig = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
        p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
        p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
        TRUE ~ ""
      ),

      p = case_when(
        p.value < 0.001 ~ "<0.001",
        TRUE ~ sprintf("%.3f", p.value)
      ),

      Beta = round(estimate, 3),
      SE = round(std.error, 3),
      t = round(statistic, 2)
    )

  # -----------------------------
  # TABLE
  # -----------------------------
  cat("<h4>", biomarker, " - ", group_name, "</h4>")

  print(
    tidy_df %>%
      select(Group, term, Beta, SE, t, p, Sig) %>%
      kable("html", escape = FALSE) %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  return(m1)
}


biomarkers <- c("log_pTau217", "log_NFL", "log_GFAP")

results <- list()

for (b in biomarkers) {

  for (g in levels(df_v3$birth_timing)) {

    cat("<hr>")
    cat("<h3>", b, " | ", g, "</h3>")

    results[[paste(b, g)]] <- run_stratified_model(
      df_v3,
      biomarker = b,
      group_name = g
    )
  }
}

```

<span style=font-weight:bold;">
BIOMARKERS: BY VISIT (dividing birth in prebirth/postbirth/NA plasma acquisition)
</span>

```{r ga plotall3 prepostblood, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
############### Dividing V3 depending on the blood acquisition

df_v3 <- long_df %>%
  filter(Visit == "v3") %>%
  mutate(
    v3_phase = factor(
      bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("v3.1 Before birth", "v3.2 After birth")
    )
  )
df_v3 <- df_v3 %>%
  mutate(
    v3_phase = case_when(
      bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y == 0 ~ "Before birth",
      bl_time.col.del.2cat_m_0y == 1 ~ "After birth",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    ),
    v3_phase = factor(
      v3_phase,
      levels = c("Before birth", "After birth")
    )
  )

df_all_split <- long_df %>%
  filter(Visit != "v3") %>%
  bind_rows(df_v3) %>%
  mutate(
    Visit = factor(Visit, levels = c("v1", "v2", "v3", "v4"))
  )

bio_colors_v3 <- list(
  log_pTau217 = list(
    base    = "#1b9e77",
    v3_1    = "#a6dba0",
    v3_2    = "#a6dba0",
    missing = "#a6dba0"
  ),

  log_NFL = list(
    base    = "#d95f02",
    v3_1    = "#fdd0a2",
    v3_2    = "#fdd0a2",
    missing = "#fdd0a2"
  ),

  log_GFAP = list(
    base    = "#7570b3",
    v3_1    = "#cbc9e2",
    v3_2    = "#cbc9e2",
    missing = "#cbc9e2"
  )
)
############### BOXPLOTS Dividing V3 depending on the blood acquisition
library(forcats)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

plot_biomarker_visits_v3split <- function(df, biomarker){

  cols <- bio_colors_v3[[biomarker]]

  df %>%
    mutate(

      # ----------------------------
      # CLEAN V3 LABELS FIRST
      # ----------------------------
      v3_phase_clean = case_when(
        Visit == "v3" & v3_phase == "v3.1 Before birth" ~ "Before birth",
        Visit == "v3" & v3_phase == "v3.2 After birth"  ~ "After birth",
        Visit == "v3" & is.na(v3_phase)                 ~ "Missing",
        TRUE ~ as.character(Visit)
      ),

      # force proper ordering + clean factor levels
      Visit_plot = factor(
        v3_phase_clean,
        levels = c(
          "v1",
          "v2",
          "Before birth",
          "After birth",
          "Missing",
          "v4"
        )
      )
    ) %>%

    ggplot(aes(x = Visit_plot, y = .data[[biomarker]])) +

    geom_boxplot(
      aes(fill = Visit_plot),
      alpha = 0.4,
      outlier.alpha = 0.2,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ) +

    geom_jitter(
      aes(color = Visit_plot),
      width = 0.15,
      alpha = 0.5,
      size = 1,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ) +

    scale_fill_manual(values = c(
      "v1" = cols$base,
      "v2" = cols$base,
      "v4" = cols$base,
      "Before birth" = cols$v3_1,
      "After birth"  = cols$v3_2,
      "Missing"      = cols$missing
    )) +

    scale_color_manual(values = c(
      "v1" = cols$base,
      "v2" = cols$base,
      "v4" = cols$base,
      "Before birth" = cols$v3_1,
      "After birth"  = cols$v3_2,
      "Missing"      = cols$missing
    )) +

    guides(
      fill = "none",
      color = "none"
    ) +

    theme_minimal() +

    labs(
      title = paste(biomarker, "across visits (V3 split)"),
      x = "Visit",
      y = biomarker
    ) +

    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1)
    )
}
############### SPAGHETTI PLOT Dividing V3 depending on the blood acquisition
plot_spaghetti_v3split <- function(df, biomarker){

  df %>%
    
    mutate(
      v3_phase = factor(
        ifelse(Visit == "v3", as.character(v3_phase), NA),
        levels = c("v3.1 Before birth", "v3.2 After birth")
      )
    ) %>%
    
    ggplot(
      aes(
        x = GA_weeks,
        y = .data[[biomarker]],
        group = id_bisc
      )
    ) +

    # individual trajectories
    geom_line(
      aes(color = v3_phase),
      alpha = 0.25,
      linewidth = 0.5,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ) +

    geom_point(
      aes(color = v3_phase),
      alpha = 0.5,
      size = 1.5,
      na.rm = TRUE
    ) +

    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(
        "v3.1 Before birth" = "#1f77b4",
        "v3.2 After birth"  = "#d62728"
      ),
      na.value = "grey70"
    ) +

    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +

    labs(
      title = paste0(biomarker, " trajectories by GA weeks (V3 split)"),
      x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
      y = biomarker,
      color = "V3 phase"
    ) +

    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
      legend.position = "right"
    )
}
########################################################## TIME between blood acquisition and delivery

plot_biomarker_delivery_timing <- function(df, biomarker){

  df %>%
    mutate(
      delivery_phase = case_when(
        bl_time.col.del_m_0y < 0 ~ "Before birth",
        bl_time.col.del_m_0y >= 0 ~ "After birth"
      ),

      delivery_phase = factor(delivery_phase,
                              levels = c("Before birth", "After birth"))
    ) %>%

    ggplot(aes(x = bl_time.col.del_m_0y,
               y = .data[[biomarker]])) +

    # reference line at delivery
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0,
               linetype = "dashed",
               color = "grey50") +

    # scatter points colored by sign
    geom_point(
      aes(color = delivery_phase),
      alpha = 0.5,
      size = 1.5
    ) +

    # smooth trend (optional but useful here)
    geom_smooth(
      aes(color = delivery_phase),
      se = FALSE,
      linewidth = 1
    ) +

    scale_color_manual(values = c(
      "Before birth" = "#2c7bb6",  # blue (pre-birth)
      "After birth"  = "#d7191c"   # red (post-birth)
    )) +

    guides(color = "none") +

    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +

    labs(
      title = paste(biomarker, "vs time to delivery"),
      x = "Time from delivery (h)",
      y = biomarker
    )
}
####################################################### ANALYZING TIME DIFFERENCES
long_df <- long_df %>%
  mutate(
    timing_group = case_when(
      bl_time.col.del_m_0y <= -20 ~ "Early prenatal",
      bl_time.col.del_m_0y > -20 & bl_time.col.del_m_0y < 0 ~ "Late prenatal",
      bl_time.col.del_m_0y >= 0 & bl_time.col.del_m_0y <= 20 ~ "Early postnatal",
      bl_time.col.del_m_0y > 20 ~ "Late postnatal"
    )
  )

long_df$timing_group <- factor(
  long_df$timing_group,
  levels = c(
    "Early prenatal",
    "Late prenatal",
    "Early postnatal",
    "Late postnatal"
  )
)

analyze_timing_groups_html <- function(df, biomarker){

  library(dplyr)
  library(broom)
  library(lmtest)
  library(sandwich)
  library(emmeans)
  library(knitr)
  library(kableExtra)

  cat("\n============================\n")
  cat("Biomarker:", biomarker, "\n")
  cat("============================\n")

  # ----------------------------
  # 1. Group sizes
  # ----------------------------
  n_tab <- table(df$timing_group)

  n_df <- as.data.frame(n_tab)
  colnames(n_df) <- c("Group", "N")

  print(
    kable(n_df, caption = "Sample size per timing group") %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  if(min(n_tab) < 10){
    cat("\n⚠️ Warning: at least one group has very small sample size (<10)\n")
  }

  # ----------------------------
  # 2. Global model
  # ----------------------------
  formula <- as.formula(paste(biomarker, "~ timing_group"))
  model <- lm(formula, data = df)

  global_test <- car::Anova(model, type = 2)

  cat("\n Global test (Type II ANOVA)\n")
  print(
    kable(as.data.frame(global_test), caption = "ANOVA") %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  p_global <- global_test$`Pr(>F)`[1]

  # ----------------------------
  # 3. Post-hoc
  # ----------------------------
  posthoc_table <- NULL

  emm <- emmeans(model, ~ timing_group)
  posthoc <- pairs(emm, adjust = "tukey")

  posthoc_table <- as.data.frame(posthoc)

  # ----------------------------
  # ADD SIGNIFICANCE STARS
  # ----------------------------
  posthoc_table$signif <- cut(
    posthoc_table$p.value,
    breaks = c(-Inf, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, Inf),
    labels = c("***", "**", "*", "")
  )

  # optional: flag small-group comparisons
  posthoc_table$note <- ifelse(
    grepl("Late postnatal", posthoc_table$contrast),
    "⚠ low n group",
    ""
  )

  print(
    kable(posthoc_table,
          digits = 4,
          caption = "Post-hoc pairwise comparisons (Tukey-adjusted)") %>%
      kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
  )

  # ----------------------------
  # 4. Return object
  # ----------------------------
  return(list(
    model = model,
    global_test = global_test,
    posthoc = posthoc_table,
    n_by_group = n_tab
  ))
}

```

##### pTau217 {.tabset}

```{r v3management_ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
res_ptau <- run_birth_timing_model(df_v3, "log_pTau217")
plot_biomarker_visits_v3split(df_all_split, "log_pTau217")
plot_spaghetti_v3split(df_all_split, "log_pTau217")
plot_biomarker_delivery_timing(df_all_split, "log_pTau217")
analyze_timing_groups_html(long_df, "log_pTau217")
```

##### NfL {.tabset}

```{r v3management nfl, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
res_nfl  <- run_birth_timing_model(df_v3, "log_NFL")
plot_biomarker_visits_v3split(df_all_split, "log_NFL")
plot_spaghetti_v3split(df_all_split, "log_NFL")
plot_biomarker_delivery_timing(df_all_split, "log_NFL")
analyze_timing_groups_html(long_df, "log_NFL")
```

##### GFAP {.tabset}

```{r v3management gfap, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
res_gfap <- run_birth_timing_model(df_v3, "log_GFAP")
plot_biomarker_visits_v3split(df_all_split, "log_GFAP")
plot_spaghetti_v3split(df_all_split, "log_GFAP")
plot_biomarker_delivery_timing(df_all_split, "log_GFAP")
```


#### Preeclampsia and angiogenic factors {.tabset}

```{r preeclampsia ptau217, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

long_df_tmp <- long_df %>%
  mutate(
    C_pree_num = case_when(
      C_pree %in% c(1, "1", "Yes", "yes") ~ 1,
      C_pree %in% c(0, "0", "No", "no") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),

    C_peeg_num = case_when(
      C_peeg %in% c(1, "1", "Yes", "yes") ~ 1,
      C_peeg %in% c(0, "0", "No", "no") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),

    C_hel_num = case_when(
      C_hel %in% c(1, "1", "Yes", "yes") ~ 1,
      C_hel %in% c(0, "0", "No", "no") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),

    pe_total_num = case_when(
      pe_total_m %in% c(1, "1", "Yes", "yes") ~ 1,
      pe_total_m %in% c(0, "0", "No", "no") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    ),

    hdp_denovo_num = case_when(
      hdp_denovo_m %in% c(1, "1", "Yes", "yes") ~ 1,
      hdp_denovo_m %in% c(0, "0", "No", "no") ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ NA_real_
    )
  )

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

# =========================================================
# 1. PREECLAMPSIA DEFINITION (SAFE, ONCE ONLY)
# =========================================================

long_df_tmp <- long_df_tmp %>%
  mutate(
    preeclampsia = case_when(
      C_pree_num == 1 ~ 1,
      C_peeg_num == 1 ~ 1,
      C_hel_num  == 1 ~ 1,
      pe_total_num == 1 ~ 1,
      hdp_denovo_num == 1 ~ 1,
      TRUE ~ 0
    )
  )

# =========================================================
# 2. CHECK CASES
# =========================================================

cat("Preeclampsia distribution")

table(long_df_tmp$preeclampsia) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  kable("html") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

# =========================================================
# 3. SINGLE BIOMARKER ANALYSIS
# =========================================================

biomarker_var <- "sflt1.plgf_m_32w_log2"

df <- long_df_tmp %>%
  select(Visit, id_bisc, preeclampsia, all_of(biomarker_var)) %>%
  mutate(
    value = as.numeric(.data[[biomarker_var]])
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(value))

# -----------------------------
# Summary table (single biomarker)
# -----------------------------
summary_table_1 <- df %>%
  group_by(Visit, preeclampsia) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    Mean = mean(value),
    SD = sd(value),
    Median = median(value),
    IQR = IQR(value),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Group = ifelse(preeclampsia == 1, "PE", "No PE")) %>%
  select(Visit, Group, N, Mean, SD, Median, IQR)

# -----------------------------
# p-values (safe)
# -----------------------------
p_values <- df %>%
  group_by(Visit) %>%
  summarise(
    p_value = tryCatch(
      wilcox.test(value ~ preeclampsia)$p.value,
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

summary_table_1 <- summary_table_1 %>%
  left_join(p_values, by = "Visit")

# -----------------------------
# TABLE
# -----------------------------
cat("Biomarker vs PE (by Visit) - angiogenic factors: log2 ratio of sFlt-1 and PlGF (higher ratio -> higher probability of preeclampsia)")

summary_table_1 %>%
  mutate(p_value = signif(p_value, 3)) %>%
  kable("html", digits = 3) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

# -----------------------------
# PLOT (single biomarker)
# -----------------------------
ggplot(df, aes(x = factor(preeclampsia), y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = factor(preeclampsia))) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, alpha = 0.4) +
  facet_wrap(~ Visit) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("No PE", "PE")) +
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "log2 ratio sFlt-1 / PlGF",
    title = "sFlt-1/PlGF ratio by preeclampsia status"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# =========================================================
# 4. MULTI-BIOMARKER ANALYSIS
# =========================================================

bio_colors <- c(
  "log_pTau217" = "#1b9e77",
  "log_NFL"     = "#d95f02",
  "log_GFAP"    = "#7570b3"
)

df_bio <- long_df_tmp %>%
  select(id_bisc, Visit, preeclampsia,
         log_pTau217, log_NFL, log_GFAP) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(log_pTau217, log_NFL, log_GFAP),
    names_to = "biomarker",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(value = as.numeric(value)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(value))

# -----------------------------
# Summary table
# -----------------------------
summary_table_2 <- df_bio %>%
  group_by(biomarker, preeclampsia) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    Mean = mean(value),
    SD = sd(value),
    Median = median(value),
    IQR = IQR(value),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(Group = ifelse(preeclampsia == 1, "PE", "No PE")) %>%
  select(biomarker, Group, N, Mean, SD, Median, IQR)

# -----------------------------
# p-values
# -----------------------------
p_vals <- df_bio %>%
  group_by(biomarker) %>%
  summarise(
    p_value = tryCatch(
      wilcox.test(value ~ preeclampsia)$p.value,
      error = function(e) NA_real_
    ),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

summary_table_2 <- summary_table_2 %>%
  left_join(p_vals, by = "biomarker")

# -----------------------------
# TABLE
# -----------------------------

cat("Biomarkers vs PE (preeclampsia group not measured with angiogenic factors)")

summary_table_2 %>%
  mutate(p_value = signif(p_value, 3)) %>%
  kable("html", digits = 3) %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

# -----------------------------
# PLOT (multi biomarker)
# -----------------------------
ggplot(df_bio, aes(x = factor(preeclampsia), y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(fill = biomarker),
               outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_jitter(aes(color = biomarker),
              width = 0.15, alpha = 0.5, size = 1) +
  facet_wrap(biomarker ~ Visit, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = bio_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = bio_colors) +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = c("No PE", "PE")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(legend.position = "none")

# =========================================================
# 5. SPAGHETTI PLOT (PE ONLY)
# =========================================================

df_pe <- df_bio %>%
  filter(preeclampsia == 1)

ggplot(df_pe, aes(x = Visit, y = value, group = id_bisc, color = id_bisc)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.8, size = 1.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ biomarker, scales = "free_y") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Spaghetti plots — PE cases (colored by participant)",
    x = "Visit",
    y = "Biomarker value"
  )

# =========================================================
# 6. SPAGHETTI PLOT (PE AND NO PE)
# =========================================================
df_spag <- df_bio %>%
  mutate(
    Visit = factor(Visit, levels = c("v1", "v2", "v3", "v4")),
    preeclampsia = factor(preeclampsia, levels = c(0, 1),
                          labels = c("No PE", "PE")),
    id_bisc = factor(id_bisc)
  )

ggplot(df_spag, aes(x = Visit, y = value, group = id_bisc)) +
  geom_line(aes(color = preeclampsia), alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_point(aes(color = preeclampsia), alpha = 0.7, size = 1.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ biomarker, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("No PE" = "#4D4D4D", "PE" = "#D95F02")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Longitudinal trajectories: PE vs No PE",
    x = "Visit",
    y = "Biomarker value"
  )

```


#### Prematurity {.tabset}

```{r prematurity, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE,fig.show='hold', fig.keep='all'}
############################################################
# PRETERM vs TERM ANALYSIS
############################################################

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

# =========================================================
# 1. CREATE PRETERM VARIABLE
# =========================================================
# Standard definition:
# Preterm birth = delivery before 37 completed weeks

long_df <- long_df %>%

  group_by(id_bisc) %>%

  mutate(

    # delivery GA from v3
    delivery_ga =
      ifelse(any(Visit == "v3"),
             GA_weeks[Visit == "v3"][1],
             NA),

    preterm =
      case_when(
        delivery_ga < 37 ~ "Preterm",
        delivery_ga >= 37 ~ "Term",
        TRUE ~ NA_character_
      )

  ) %>%

  ungroup()

# =========================================================
# 2. SHOW N
# =========================================================
cat("\n====================================================\n")
cat("PRETERM COUNTS\n")
cat("====================================================\n\n")

table_preterm <-
  long_df %>%
  distinct(id_bisc, preterm) %>%
  count(preterm)

print(table_preterm)

# =========================================================
# 3. BIOMARKERS
# =========================================================
biomarkers <- c(
  "log_GFAP",
  "log_NFL",
  "log_pTau217"
)

# =========================================================
# 4. BOXPLOTS AT v3 AND v4
# =========================================================
for (bio in biomarkers) {

  cat("\n====================================================\n")
  cat("BOXPLOTS:", bio, "\n")
  cat("====================================================\n\n")

  df_plot <-
    long_df %>%
    filter(Visit %in% c("v3", "v4")) %>%
    filter(!is.na(preterm))

  p_box <- ggplot(
    df_plot,
    aes(
      x = preterm,
      y = .data[[bio]],
      fill = preterm
    )
  ) +

    geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.6, outlier.shape = NA) +

    geom_jitter(
      width = 0.12,
      alpha = 0.35
    ) +

    facet_wrap(~Visit) +

    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +

    labs(
      title = paste0(bio, ": Preterm vs Term"),
      x = "",
      y = bio
    ) +

    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none"
    )

  print(p_box)
}

# =========================================================
# 5. SPAGHETTI PLOTS (v1-v3)
# =========================================================
for (bio in biomarkers) {

  cat("\n====================================================\n")
  cat("SPAGHETTI PLOT:", bio, "\n")
  cat("====================================================\n\n")

  df_spag <-
    long_df %>%
    filter(Visit %in% c("v1", "v2", "v3")) %>%
    filter(!is.na(preterm))

  # -------------------------------------------------------
  # INDIVIDUAL TRAJECTORIES
  # -------------------------------------------------------
  p_spag <- ggplot(
    df_spag,
    aes(
      x = GA_weeks,
      y = .data[[bio]],
      group = id_bisc,
      color = preterm
    )
  ) +

    geom_line(alpha = 0.18, linewidth = 0.5) +

    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +

    labs(
      title = paste0(bio, " trajectories during pregnancy"),
      x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
      y = bio,
      color = ""
    ) +

    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

  # -------------------------------------------------------
  # GROUP TREND ONLY
  # -------------------------------------------------------
  p_pred <- ggplot(
    df_spag,
    aes(
      x = GA_weeks,
      y = .data[[bio]],
      color = preterm
    )
  ) +

    geom_smooth(
      method = "loess",
      se = TRUE,
      linewidth = 1.8
    ) +

    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +

    labs(
      title = paste0(bio, " predicted trajectories"),
      x = "Gestational age (weeks)",
      y = bio,
      color = ""
    ) +

    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

  print(p_spag)
  print(p_pred)
}
```


### 2.4. Cognition and behaviour {.tabset}

TBD
